description: Post-review fixes: address security, crash, and correctness issues found in code review. Fixes write_file path traversal, SQLite null dereference, cloud LLM bypassing token budget, and JSON parser nested structure handling.
steps: Fix isWritePathAllowed() in tool_executor.cpp: use realpath() to canonicalize paths, fix /tmp prefix to /tmp/, reject relative paths that resolve outside allowed directories
Fix SQLite null column dereference in brain_memory.cpp: add null checks before constructing std::string from sqlite3_column_text()
Fix cloud LLM path in conversation_loop.cpp callLLM(): use the already-built prompt from MKContextBuilder instead of constructing a new one
Fix parseArgsObject() in tool_registry.cpp: add brace/bracket depth tracking to handle nested JSON values
Verify thread-safety gap is already addressed (REPL path holds systemMutex)
Run make and make test to verify all changes pass
acceptance_criteria: isWritePathAllowed rejects /tmpevil/payload, resolves symlinks, and properly handles relative paths
Cloud LLM path uses the context-builder's assembled prompt
parseArgsObject handles nested JSON objects and arrays
findings: All 5 review issues addressed. Issue #1 (path traversal): rewrote isWritePathAllowed to use realpath() for canonicalization, trailing slash on /tmp/, and resolution of relative paths. Issue #2 (SQLite null): added null-checks on both loadFromDisk and queryRelevantMemories. Issue #3 (cloud LLM budget): cloud path now receives the same prompt string the ContextBuilder assembled. Issue #4 (thread-safety): confirmed already fixed - REPL path at line 2129 acquires systemMutex before calling handle_natural_query. Issue #5 (JSON parser): added brace/bracket depth tracking with string-skip logic for nested structures. Build: 6 warnings (all pre-existing). Tests: 80 passed, 0 failed, 460 assertions.
